give me statusline proposals for this proejct for claude code -- give me 10 ascii examples of how you would render it

Here are 10 statusline proposals tailored to tenex-edge — a mix of standard Claude Code data (model, branch, context) and fabric-specific data this project uniquely has (`tenex-edge who`, `tenex-edge inbox`, session id, daemon state).

**1. Minimal classic** — model, branch, dirty marker
```
Fable 5 │ tenex-edge │  inbox-envelope* 
```

**2. Fabric-aware** — adds peer count and unread mentions from the daemon
```
Fable 5 │  inbox-envelope* │ ⬡ 9 peers │ ✉ 2 unread
```

**3. Session identity** — leads with who *this* agent is on the fabric
```
claude@tenex-edge [601a36] │ Fable 5 │  inbox-envelope* │ ✉ 0
```

**4. Context-budget focused** — for long sessions, shows context burn
```
Fable 5 │ inbox-envelope* │ ctx ▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░ 48% │ $1.27
```

**5. Full dashboard** — everything, single dense line
```
⬡ claude@tenex-edge:601a36 │ Fable 5 │  inbox-envelope ✚7 ●21 │ ✉2 │ ctx 48% │ 14:32
```
(`✚7` = untracked files, `●21` = modified — matches your current git status shape)

**6. Daemon health** — surfaces whether the edge daemon/relay link is alive
```
Fable 5 │ inbox-envelope* │ daemon ● up │ relay ● 3/3 │ ✉ 2
```

**7. Two-tone semantic** — words only when something needs attention, otherwise quiet
```
Fable 5 │ inbox-envelope*                          ⚠ 2 mentions waiting
```

**8. Rust-project flavored** — adds last `cargo check` result (cached by a hook)
```
Fable 5 │ inbox-envelope* │ cargo ✓ │ tests 41/41 │ ✉ 0
```

**9. Fleet view** — shows what the busiest sibling sessions are doing
```
Fable 5 │ inbox-envelope* │ fleet: 8 idle, 1 active (f18a67: dispatch logic)
```

**10. Powerline style** — segmented with arrows, classic terminal aesthetic
```
 Fable 5  inbox-envelope ±21  ⬡ 9  ✉ 2  48% ctx 
```
(in practice rendered with background colors per segment)

A few implementation notes:

- The statusline script receives JSON on stdin with `model.display_name`, `workspace.current_dir`, `cost.total_cost_usd`, and context info — segments 1–5 need nothing else.
- Fabric segments (peers, inbox, fleet) would shell out to `tenex-edge who` / `tenex-edge inbox`. Since the statusline re-runs frequently, you'd want to cache those to a temp file every ~10s rather than hitting the daemon on every render — or better, have the daemon write a tiny status file the statusline just `cat`s.
- My pick: **#3 or #5**. Session identity in the statusline is genuinely useful in this project because you regularly run many sibling sessions and the `[601a36]`-style id is how the fabric addresses them.

Want me to implement one? I can write the script and wire it into `.claude/settings.json`.

Right — having read the wiki, the statusline shouldn't be a generic git/model bar. The project's whole thesis is **citizenship**: durable identity (`slug@hostname`), presence in a NIP-29 project group, an inbox of mentions, a fleet you're aware of, and provenance. The statusline is essentially a one-line awareness board — the "floor" product (awareness + identity) rendered in the host. Here are 10 proposals, each anchored to a specific concept from the docs:

**1. Citizenship line** — who am I on the fabric, full identity chain: agent@host, project group, session short code
```
pablo ▸ claude@kubrick ⌗601a36 · h:tenex-edge · ♥12s
```
(`♥12s` = time since my last presence heartbeat published — the 30s kind-30315 loop is alive)

**2. Membership status** — surfaces the NIP-29 membership warning *persistently*, not just on first turn (the docs record an LLM ignoring the injected warning; a statusline can't be ignored)
```
claude@kubrick ⌗601a36 · ⚠ NOT IN GROUP tenex-edge — run: tenex-edge project add
```
and when healthy:
```
claude@kubrick ⌗601a36 · h:tenex-edge ✓member · ✉0
```

**3. Roster delta** — mirrors what turn-start injects (deltas since last turn: joins, leaves, status changes), so you see fabric motion *between* turns
```
⌗601a36 · 6 peers │ +codex@tenex-edge joined · f18a67→"editing dispatch logic"
```

**4. Collision watch** — the Rung-0 collision logger as a live signal: a sibling session is active in a path I've touched this session
```
⌗601a36 · h:tenex-edge · ⚠ f18a67 also in src/daemon/server/ · ✉1
```

**5. Inbox envelope** — the email-metaphor inbox, newest sender surfaced (From/age, matching the envelope redesign)
```
claude@kubrick · ✉2 ‹ codex@tenex-edge 3m · claude@kubrick:f18a67 11m ›
```

**6. Substrate health** — the failure modes the docs actually record: daemon (UDS), relay, heartbeat staleness, parallel-daemon hazard
```
daemon● sock ok · relay● nip29.f7z.io · ♥8s · db wal ✓     ⌗601a36
```
degraded (fail-open, like the host adapters):
```
daemon○ unreachable — fabric blind, host unimpeded          ⌗601a36
```

**7. Fleet topology** — local vs remote machines, since identity is machine-bound and `who` now shows hostnames
```
fleet: 4 kubrick · 1 codex · 3 hetzner⇡ │ 1 working · ✉0 · ⌗601a36
```

**8. Provenance trail** — every turn publishes a signed TurnReply; show the last signed event and that the scrubber ran
```
⌗601a36 · last turn-reply 2m ago (kind:1 signed, scrub✓) · thread root e4f9…
```

**9. ACL attention** — pending foreign agents p-tagging the owner, awaiting allow/block (the one thing only the human can decide)
```
claude@kubrick · h:tenex-edge · ⚑ 1 pending agent → tenex-edge acl
```

**10. Quiet citizenship** — minimal when all is well, loud only on the four attention states (no membership, inbox, collision, ACL pending)
```
claude@kubrick ⌗601a36 ♥
```
vs.
```
claude@kubrick ⌗601a36 ♥ · ✉2 · ⚠collision src/state/ · ⚑acl
```

Implementation notes anchored to the architecture:

- All of this is one read from the daemon over the UDS JSON-RPC — a natural new verb like `tenex-edge statusline --json` (or reusing the read model behind `who` + `peek_inbox`). Critically it must be **pure-read like turn-check** (`peek_inbox`, no state.db writes), since Claude Code re-runs the statusline constantly and you must not reintroduce transient concurrent writers.
- It must **fail open** like the host adapters: daemon down → render proposal 6's degraded line (or nothing), never block or error.
- The statusline is arguably the *right* surface for the membership warning false-positive problem: unlike injected context, it self-corrects as the cache refreshes.

My pick: **#10 as the frame** (quiet when healthy) with #2's membership state and #4's collision watch as its attention states — that's literally the floor product (identity + awareness + passive collision signal) in one line. claude@host [session-id] #[agent-symbol] #[sessions-symbol] [current-activity] [codex@host: Please review my work]


                         ^ count of agents  ^ sessions active ^ what this session ^ message in the current inbox (either recently consumed (30 seconds ago) or to be consumed)
                           that are members   (including idle)  is saying its doing

That format maps cleanly onto the fabric model: identity, session, group roster size, live sessions, self-reported status (the presence/30315 line), and the inbox envelope. Here's how I'd render it, with a few symbol options and all the states it needs to handle.

**Symbol pair options for the two counts**

```
A)  claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ◉9 …
B)  claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ⌁9 …
C)  claude@kubrick [601a36] a:4 s:9 …
D)  claude@kubrick [601a36] 4⬡ 9⚡ …
```
- `⬡` for agents reads well — hexagon = node on the fabric, and it's the symbol the fleet already associates with tenex-edge.
- For sessions, `◉` (live dot) is the cleanest; `⌁`/`⚡` suggest heartbeat. `a:`/`s:` is the no-unicode fallback.

**The states, rendered with option A:**

Working, with a pending (unconsumed) mention:
```
claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ◉9 ✎ refactoring inbox envelope ✉ codex@kubrick: Please review my work
```

Working, mention consumed <30s ago (dimmed in practice, `✓` marks drained):
```
claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ◉9 ✎ refactoring inbox envelope ✉✓ codex@kubrick: Please review my work
```

Working, inbox empty (message segment simply absent — no `✉0` noise):
```
claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ◉9 ✎ scrubbing secrets from outgoing events
```

Idle:
```
claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ◉9 · idle
```

Multiple pending — show newest, count the rest:
```
claude@kubrick [601a36] ⬡4 ◉9 ✎ wiring statusline verb ✉2 codex@kubrick: Please review my work (+1)
```

**Semantics I'd pin down, per your annotations:**

- `⬡4` — distinct agent identities that are **members of the NIP-29 group** for this project (from the membership table the daemon hydrates), not just currently-present ones. So it's roster size, stable.
- `◉9` — sessions with a **non-expired presence heartbeat** in this project, including idle ones. This is exactly what `who` computes.
- Activity — this session's own self-reported status, i.e. the same string sibling sessions see for you in `who`. Truncate at ~40 chars with `…`.
- Inbox segment — pending message shows bright; once drained by turn-start it lingers for 30s as `✉✓` then disappears. Sender rendered as `slug@hostname` consistent with `who`. Long messages truncate at ~50 chars.

One constraint from the architecture: Claude Code re-runs the statusline very frequently, so this needs a single pure-read daemon RPC (a `statusline` verb doing roster-count + session-count + own-status + `peek_inbox`, like turn-check — zero state.db writes), and it must fail open: daemon unreachable → render just `claude@kubrick [601a36]` rather than erroring.

If this rendering looks right, I'll add the `statusline` verb to the daemon and wire the script into `.claude/settings.json`.

Done. The statusline is implemented, installed, and live-tested — one manual step remains because your settings deny me from editing `~/.claude/settings.json`.

**What was built (137 lines across 8 files, 103 tests pass, zero new clippy lints):**

- A `statusline` daemon RPC (`src/daemon/server/awareness.rs`) — one pure-read snapshot like `turn_check`: roster size from the NIP-29 `group_members` cache, live session count via `load_who_snapshot`, this session's own status + working flag, `peek_inbox` for pending mentions, and a new `delivered_at` column so drained mentions linger 30s as "recently consumed". Zero writes to state.db, since the harness calls this constantly.
- A `tenex-edge statusline` CLI verb (`src/cli/statusline.rs`) — reads Claude Code's stdin payload for `session_id`, renders the line, and fails open: daemon down → prints nothing, exits 0, and uses a new `call_no_spawn` client so it never boots a daemon just to draw a line.

with the membership warning (`⚠ not in group …`), `✉N sender@host: subject (+k)`, and `✉✓` consumed states covered by unit tests.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - tenex-edge: Rust binary grafting a Nostr-based "nervous system" onto agents in native hosts (Claude Code/Codex/opencode); "citizenship for your agents".
   - Single per-machine daemon (`tenex-edge __daemon`): sole writer of state.db (SQLite, WAL), single relay connection, JSON-RPC over Unix domain socket at $TENEX_EDGE_HOME/daemon.sock; spawn-if-absent; idle-exit; protocol version handshake (currently 2) with PleaseExit skew handling.
   - NIP-29 project groups (h tag = project slug), group_members cache (kind 39002 authoritative + optimistic 9000 put-user); membership warning if agent not in group.
   - Identity: sovereign keypair per (agent, machine), `slug@hostname` display via `slugify_host`; session short codes via `session_short_code` hash (SessionId newtype Display).
   - Presence heartbeats (kind 30315, 30s tick), PEER_FRESH_SECS=90; session-scoped status (session_status table) = "what this session is doing".
   - Inbox: per-session mention rows; `peek_inbox` (pure read, used by turn_check), `drain_inbox` (marks delivered); new `delivered_at` for "recently consumed" 30s window.
   - Constraints established: statusline RPC must be PURE-READ (no state.db writes — turn_check precedent, avoids re-introducing concurrent writers); CLI must FAIL OPEN (daemon down → print nothing, exit 0) and NEVER spawn a daemon (new `call_no_spawn`).
   - Claude Code statusline protocol: command receives JSON on stdin (`session_id`, `workspace.current_dir`, `cwd`, model); ANSI colors supported.
   - ccstatusline (github.com/sirmalloc/ccstatusline): multi-line statusline multiplexer; custom-command widgets always pipe the Claude Code stdin JSON to child commands (verified in src/widgets/CustomCommand.tsx via execSync input); config at ~/.config/ccstatusline/settings.json, `lines` = array of arrays of widgets, keys: `commandPath`, `preserveColors`, `timeout`.
   - Install flow: `just install` (release build → ~/.local/bin/tenex-edge + codesign fix), then `pkill -f 'tenex-edge __daemon'` so the fresh daemon exposes new RPCs.
   - Security constraints (verbatim, must preserve): user settings deny `"Write(~/.claude/settings.json)"` and `"Edit(~/.claude/settings.json)"` — I cannot edit that file; the user must apply the statusLine swap themselves.
